No Poverty.reason: The course highlights international trade as a 'locomotive of economic development,' which indirectly contributes to poverty reduction through economic growth (Target 1.a).

Zero Hunger.reason: The course explicitly covers '農業' (agriculture) and '食品進口管制' (food import control), directly relating to WTO rules on agricultural trade and food markets (Targets 2.b, 2.c).

Good Health and Well-being.reason: The course explicitly covers 'TRIPs,' which is directly linked to public health and access to medicines for developing countries under the Doha Declaration (Target 3.b).

Quality Education.reason: As a university course, it provides quality tertiary education (Target 4.3) and aims to equip students with skills for employment in government and multinational corporations (Target 4.4).

Affordable and Clean Energy.reason: The course explicitly discusses '我國離岸風電本土自製率要求' (offshore wind power local content requirements), directly relating to renewable energy trade policy (Targets 7.2, 7.a).

Decent Work and Economic Growth.reason: The course explicitly identifies international trade as the 'locomotive of economic development,' directly supporting sustained economic growth (Target 8.1) and productive employment (Target 8.3).

Industry, Innovation and Infrastructure.reason: The course covers '貨物貿易各項協定' and '離岸風電本土自製率要求,' linking to industrialization, market integration, and upgrading industries with clean technologies (Targets 9.2, 9.4).

Reduced Inequalities.reason: The course's focus on WTO principles, exceptions for developing countries, and reform discussions directly addresses special treatment (Target 10.a) and enhancing their voice in global institutions (Target 10.6).

Sustainable Cities and Communities.reason: No explicit or inferred evidence in the course description or objectives relates to sustainable cities and communities.

Responsible Consumption and Production.reason: The course mentions '環境' (environment) as a WTO topic. Trade rules can indirectly influence sustainable resource management and production patterns (Target 12.2).

Climate Action.reason: The course discusses '環境' (environment) and '離岸風電本土自製率要求.' Trade policies related to renewable energy contribute to integrating climate measures into national policies (Target 13.2).
evidence: 我國離岸風電本土自製率要求

Life Below Water.reason: The course's focus on WTO agreements on trade in goods implicitly includes ongoing WTO negotiations on fisheries subsidies, directly addressing marine resource management (Target 14.6).

Life on Land.reason: The course's discussion of '農業' (agriculture) and '環境' (environment) within the WTO context can indirectly relate to trade policies affecting land use and biodiversity (Targets 15.1, 15.9).

Peace, Justice and Strong Institutions.reason: The course is fundamentally about '國際貿易法,' emphasizing '法律' in global development and covering the WTO's '爭端解決機制,' aligning with rule of law and strong institutions (Targets 16.3, 16.6).

Partnerships for the Goals.reason: The course's central focus is the WTO, representing the multilateral trading system (Target 17.10). It also covers '區域貿易協定(RTAs),' explicitly demonstrating commitment to global partnerships.
